title: High Efficiency Transformation Protocol (modified)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fmv7c","text":"This is a modified protocol from the original NEB splitting the 50uL aliquot into two separate tubes for dual reactions from a single tube.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"fm9v8","text":"Transformation Protocol Variables","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":33}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"d333o","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"43i58","text":"Thawing: Cells are best thawed on ice and DNA added as soon as the last bit of ice in the tube disappears. Cells can also be thawed by hand, but warming above 0°C will decrease the transformation efficiency.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5nmti","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3dvso","text":"Incubation of DNA with Cells on Ice: For maximum transformation efficiency, cells and DNA should be incubated together on ice for 30 minutes. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 10 minutes this step is shortened.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":35}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"en57l","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3k4mh","text":"Heat Shock: Both the temperature and the timing of the heat shock step are important and specific to the transformation volume and vessel. Using the transformation tube provided, 30 seconds at 42°C is optimal.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":10}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"atlnu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"661qh","text":"Outgrowth: Outgrowth at 37°C for 1 hour is best for cell recovery and for expression of antibiotic resistance. Expect a 2-fold loss in transformation efficiency for every 15 minutes this step is shortened. SOC gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than LB medium; and incubation with shaking or rotating the tube gives 2-fold higher transformation efficiency than incubation without shaking.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"6c13v","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"1lkk0","text":"Plating: Selection plates can be used warm or cold, wet or dry without significantly affecting the transformation efficiency. However, warm, dry plates are easier to spread and allow for the most rapid colony formation.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"39hs0","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"4t4fe","text":"DNA Contaminants to Avoid","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":25}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"eskjk","text":"   Contaminant Removal Method   Detergents Ethanol precipitate   Phenol Extract with chloroform and ethanol precipitate   Ethanol or Isopropanol Dry pellet before resuspending   PEG* Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate   DNA binding proteins (e.g. Ligase) Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate   ","type":"atomic","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":3,"length":11},{"style":"bold","offset":15,"length":14}],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":356}],"data":[]},{"key":"dvond","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9q8k3","text":"* Ideally, DNA for transformation should be purified and resuspended in water or TE. However, up to 10 µl of DNA directly from a ligation mix can be used with only a two-fold loss of transformation efficiency. Where it is necessary to maximize the number of transformants (e.g. a library), a purification step, either a spin column or phenol/chloroform extraction and ethanol precipitation should be added.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"tables","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"cellsMeta":{"0_0":"","0_1":"","1_0":"","1_1":"","2_0":"","2_1":"","3_0":"","3_1":"","4_0":"","4_1":"","5_0":"","5_1":""},"colWidths":[null,null],"data":[["\u003cstrong\u003eContaminant\u003c/strong\u003e","\u003cstrong\u003eRemoval Method\u003c/strong\u003e"],["Detergents","Ethanol precipitate"],["Phenol","Extract with chloroform and ethanol precipitate"],["Ethanol or Isopropanol","Dry pellet before resuspending"],["PEG*","Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate"],["DNA binding proteins (e.g. Ligase)","Column purify or phenol/chloroform extract and ethanol precipitate"]],"guid":"E69A8440457F11E9BAE4CF38B3347E4C","legend":"","mergeCells":[],"rowHeights":[],"show_headers":true}}}}

steps: Warm SOC media to room temperature SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S
Thaw a tube of NEB 5-alpha Competent E. coli cells on ice for 10 minutes. undetermined 00:10:00 NEB 5-alpha Competent E.coli (High Efficiency) - 20x0.05 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #C2987H
Aliquot 25uL of the cells into a new tube undetermined NEB 5-alpha Competent E.coli (High Efficiency) - 20x0.05 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #C2987H
Add 0.5-2.5 µl containing 0.5 pg-50 ng of plasmid DNA to each cell mixture.
Carefully flick the tube 4-5 times to mix cells and DNA. Do not vortex.
Place the mixture on ice for 30 minutes. Do not mix. 00:30:00
Heat shock at exactly 42°C for exactly 30 seconds. Do not mix. 00:00:30
Place on ice for 5 minutes. Do not mix. 00:05:00
Pipette 475 µl of room temperature SOC into the mixture in each tube undetermined SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S
Place at 37°C for 60 minutes, shaking vigorously (250 rpm) or rotating. 01:00:00
Mix the cells thoroughly by flicking the tube and inverting. SOC Outgrowth Medium - 100 ml New England Biolabs Catalog #B9020S
Perform several 10-fold serial dilutions in SOC.
Spread 50-100 µl of each dilution onto a selection plate
creator: Harold Bien
